title: quercus copeyensis
intro: Quercus copeyensis, also known as the Copey Oak, is a species of tree in the beech family Fagaceae, native to Central America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and tannins. The health benefits of Quercus copeyensis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial and antifungal activities. Quercus copeyensis can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues.
